module-0-welcome-board-project.label: Module Project: Build a Welcome Board
module-0-welcome-board-project.summary: A club leader needs a short welcome board for the first Python meetup. Build a small program that prints a clean set of messages in the right order.

sketch0.title: Your first client: a club leader with an empty welcome board

mp-1-print-event-title.title: Print the event title
mp-1-print-event-title.prompt: The club leader wants the board heading ready first. Write Python code that prints exactly `Code Club Open House`.
mp-1-print-event-title.hint: Use one `print(...)` statement with the exact text inside quotes.

help.concept: This project grows like a real beginner task: each step starts simple, works correctly, and then adds one more useful piece.
help.hint_1: You only need one line of output in this step.
help.hint_2: Match the capitalization and spacing exactly.

mp-2-print-agenda-lines.title: Print the agenda lines
mp-2-print-agenda-lines.prompt:
Now the club leader wants three short agenda lines under the title. Print these lines exactly in this order:
Welcome
Try Python
Ask questions
mp-2-print-agenda-lines.hint: Use one `print(...)` call per line of output.

help.concept: Separate print statements are the clearest way to build a short ordered output board.
help.hint_1: There should be three output lines.
help.hint_2: Keep the line order exactly as shown.

mp-2-print-agenda-lines.starterCode: # Print the three agenda lines in order

mp-3-show-seat-count.title: Show the seat count
mp-3-show-seat-count.prompt: The room has 5 seats on one side and 3 on the other. Write Python code that prints exactly `Seats ready: 8`, and compute the `8` with the expression `5 + 3` instead of typing it directly.
mp-3-show-seat-count.hint: Put the text in one print line and let Python calculate `5 + 3`.

help.concept: A small project can mix text and simple expressions so the output stays accurate when the numbers come from the code.
help.hint_1: Use the expression `5 + 3` inside the line you print.
help.hint_2: Do not hard-code the final number by itself.

mp-3-show-seat-count.starterCode: # Print the seat message and calculate the number with 5 + 3

mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.title: Build the full welcome board
mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.prompt:
Build the finished welcome board for the meetup. Print exactly these four lines:
Welcome to Code Club
Doors open at 4 PM
Helpers ready: 6
Bring your questions!

Use the expression `2 * 3` to calculate the helper count.
mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.hint: Print one line at a time and let Python calculate the helper count.

help.concept: The final step turns several small printing skills into one polished beginner program with exact output.
help.hint_1: There are four lines in the final board.
help.hint_2: Use `2 * 3` for the helper count instead of typing `6` directly.

mp-4-build-full-welcome-board.starterCode: # Build the full welcome board below

try-module-0-welcome-board-project-sketc.title: Preview the welcome board
try-module-0-welcome-board-project-sketc.prompt: Start the board with one friendly line: print exactly `Welcome to Python Club`.

help.concept: This quick preview shows the kind of clean output the project will build.
help.hint_1: You only need one line of text for this preview.

try_module_0_welcome_board_project_sketc.title: Try it yourself
try_module_0_welcome_board_project_sketc.prompt: Start the board with one friendly line: print exactly `Welcome to Python Club`.

sketch-1.title: Your first client: a club leader with an empty welcome board
sketch-1.bodyMarkdown:
A club leader is setting up the first Python meetup and needs a small welcome board on the screen before students walk in. Nothing fancy yet, just clear output in the right order.

In this module project, you will turn the first beginner tools into one finished program. Each step starts from a stable version and adds one useful detail, so the project feels like a real build instead of disconnected drills.
